rules.statement: Manually onboard the first 10 users before opening broadly
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Skipping manual onboarding means you miss critical UX friction points, fail to build early champion relationships, and scale a broken experience that drives churn before you ever reach product-market fit.

rules.statement: As a founder, you must market your product — being 'basically done' means nothing without distribution
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: A completed product with no distribution strategy dies in obscurity. The graveyard of startups is filled with technically excellent products that nobody ever heard about. Building is 30% of the job; distribution is 70%.

rules.statement: Start with one process to automate first; do not try to document everything at once
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Attempting to automate all processes simultaneously leads to scope paralysis, shallow implementations across the board, and zero completed automations. One deep win creates momentum and a template for the next.

rules.statement: Plugins must meet strict directory structure requirements for marketplace upload
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Submitting a plugin that does not conform to marketplace directory structure results in immediate rejection, wasted review cycles, and delayed distribution. The requirements exist for interoperability and cannot be negotiated.

rules.statement: Address team fears about AI replacement directly, or the transformation will fail
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Ignoring team anxiety about AI replacing their jobs triggers passive resistance, sabotage of adoption efforts, and political backlash that kills the initiative from the inside regardless of how good the technology is.

rules.statement: The window for AI-first transformation is open now but will not last forever
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Delaying AI adoption while competitors move aggressively creates a compounding disadvantage. Early movers capture market position, train their teams, and build institutional knowledge that late entrants cannot replicate quickly.

heuristics.condition: IF a client has technically savvy team members
heuristics.action: THEN get them as champions early to push adoption internally
heuristics.reason: Internal champions reduce resistance to new tools, provide real-time feedback during rollout, translate technical concepts for non-technical colleagues, and advocate for continued investment when budget reviews occur.

heuristics.condition: IF a business lacks a technical team
heuristics.action: THEN set up a retainer model to maintain the AI system on their behalf
heuristics.reason: Non-technical teams cannot troubleshoot integrations, API changes, or prompt drift. A retainer ensures the system stays functional, creates predictable recurring revenue, and prevents the client from churning due to unresolved technical issues.

heuristics.condition: IF eliminating 30-40 hours/week of manual work through automation
heuristics.action: THEN the result yields more output, more revenue, and fewer resources consumed
heuristics.reason: Reclaiming 30-40 hours per week from manual tasks unlocks capacity equivalent to a full-time hire. The freed bandwidth can be redirected to revenue-generating activities, strategic work, or headcount reduction depending on business goals.

frameworks.name: Team AI Adoption
steps: Start small by identifying the team's single biggest bottleneck or most time-consuming manual task
Build and share the automation solution with the team, demonstrating the immediate time savings
Have each team member install an AI-powered IDE (Cursor, Claude Code, or equivalent)
Set up GitHub or equivalent version control for team synchronization and collaboration
Deploy production workflows via VPS, n8n, or equivalent infrastructure for reliable operation
Iterate based on team feedback and expand automation to additional workflows

frameworks.name: Client Delivery Full Loop
steps: Audit the client's existing business processes to identify pain points and automation opportunities
Develop a strategy document mapping each pain point to a proposed AI-powered solution with ROI projections
Build the solution using the agreed-upon architecture, tools, and integrations
Train the technical team on system operation, maintenance, and troubleshooting
Train power users on day-to-day interaction with the new system
Find internal champions who are enthusiastic about the technology and can drive peer adoption
Execute change management across the broader organization, addressing fears and resistance directly
Transition to a retainer model for ongoing support, monitoring, optimization, and iteration

frameworks.name: Plugin Distribution
steps: Build the plugin to solve a specific, repeatable problem with a clean interface
Test thoroughly in isolation and with representative user workflows
Package as a zip file conforming to marketplace directory structure requirements
Upload to the target marketplace and submit for review
Conduct second-phase testing in the marketplace environment to catch integration issues
Connect MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoints if the plugin exposes AI-accessible capabilities
Iterate based on marketplace reviews, usage analytics, and user feedback

metrics.name: Staff hours automated by AI-adopting companies
Non-coders as fastest growing Claude Code user segment
Waitlist demand before early access launch
metrics.value: Hundreds of people on waitlist before early access

metrics.name: Development time compression with AI tooling
metrics.value: 4-5 months reduced to nearly finished generation
